heavyai/heavydb.note: GPU queries run via a runtime LLVM NVVM->NVPTX codegen pipeline (NativeCodegen.cpp lookupTarget(nvptx64)/triple nvptx64-nvidia-cuda/nvvm.annotations/libdevice) JIT-linked to cubin by the CUDA driver (NvidiaKernel.cpp cuLink*) and launched via the CUDA driver API. No static GPU kernel to translate and no isolated GPU unit test -> no correctness-first slice. A ROCm port needs a new LLVM-AMDGPU codegen backend + ROCm device-libs link + hipRTC/comgr runtime-compile + a port of ~70 CUDA-driver-coupled files and the warpcore CG hash table: upstream-engineering scale. The analogous Intel SPIR-V+Level-Zero backend (PRs 671/683/688-691/697) was attempted and closed unmerged; only an inert L0 memory-manager stub remains. Not a tractable mechanical MOAT port; not GPU-validatable in-pipeline. See projects/heavydb/plan.md.

The open Apache-2.0 repo contains only Python frontends. A ROCm port would require greenfield kernel reimplementation, not CUDA->HIP translation.

nvidia/cutlass.note: AMD equivalent is Composable Kernel (CK) for GEMM/attention/conv kernels, rocWMMA for tensor core abstractions, and FlyDSL for CuTe-style Python DSL. CUTLASS is deeply tied to NVIDIA PTX inline assembly (~3100 asm blocks) and tensor core ISA (mma.sync, ldmatrix) which have no HIP equivalent. See projects/cutlass/plan.md for full assessment.

open-mmlab/mmcv.note: mmcv already has mature, upstream-merged ROCm/HIP support: MMCV_WITH_HIP guards across all 58 CUDA kernels (warp size 64, __shfl_down vs __shfl_down_sync, etc.), setup.py detects ROCm PyTorch via torch.version.hip and builds the CUDAExtension path with -DMMCV_WITH_HIP (torch hipifies at build time), and docs/en/get_started/build.md documents the AMD ROCm build. PR1206 refactored mmcv/ops/csrc explicitly to support AMD GPUs. is_rocm_pytorch helpers in Python ops. No port needed; would duplicate AMD/upstream work.

opendrivelab/lightwheelocc.note: Synthetic dataset project, not compute library

Active CI/wheel pipelines maintained by PennyLaneAI/Xanadu. Docker target wheel-lightning-kokkos-rocm. Demonstrated at scale on Frontier/LUMI with MI250X GPUs.

rusty1s/pytorch_cluster.note: Upstream already merged ROCm build support: PR #149 'Enable ROCm build' (MERGED 2022-10-17) added the torch.version.hip->USE_ROCM + build-time hipify path (setup.py L19,80-84,125-126; csrc/version.cpp), present in tree today. The .cu kernels carry ZERO fault classes (no warpSize/__shfl/__ballot -- only __syncthreads tree reductions; no cub/thrust/cuBLAS/cuFFT; no textures/PTX; only device-API cuRAND in rw_cuda.cu which torch hipify maps cleanly to hipRAND/rocRAND), so no source delta exists to contribute -- the kernels build/run unmodified via torch hipify, which is why #149 changed no .cu. Maintained community ROCm wheels exist (Looong01/pyg-rocm-build, torch-cluster-rocm on PyPI; track ROCm 7.2.2/PyTorch 2.11, Apr 2026) building the unmodified source. Repo is deprecated in favor of pyg-lib (#271) and AMD states PyG is not on its ROCm roadmap (ROCm/ROCm #3655). An upstream PR would be a no-op into a deprecated repo.

rusty1s/pytorch_scatter.note: Mature AUTHORITATIVE merged upstream ROCm support. setup.py L19/L78-82 has the torch.version.hip build-time hipify path + USE_ROCM define + __HIP_NO_HALF_CONVERSIONS__ undef. csrc/cuda/utils.cuh is a USE_ROCM-guarded warp-shuffle shim (maskless __shfl_up/__shfl_down + at::Half overloads + warp_mask_t 64-bit). atomics.cuh + version.cpp ROCm-guarded. Merged PRs: #325 (ROCm org, enable_rocm, 2022-09) foundational; #411 (ashwinma/AMD, 2024-01) at::Half shfl; #498 (Looong01, 2025-08) ROCm 6.4.3+. Our own jeffdaily PR #493 was CLOSED (superseded). Maintained ROCm wheels: Looong01/pyg-rocm-build, latest 2026-04-21, ROCm up to 7.2.2, torch 2.11, py3.10-3.14. segment_coo/segment_csr warp reductions are wave-relative (TB<=32, contiguity-guarded, atomic flush) and wave64-correct as shipped. No unported fault class. Same posture as sibling mmcv + pytorch_cluster skips.

sakanaai/sparser-faster-llms.note: TwELL CUDA kernels use NVIDIA Hopper PTX (WGMMA, TMA, thread clusters) with no AMD equivalent. Requires ground-up rewrite, not a port.

scverse/rapids-singlecell.note: Built on RAPIDS (cudf/cuml); ROCm support via ROCm-DS when deps complete

serhii-rieznik/etx-tracer.note: Ray tracer uses OptiX (nvidia-specific RT cores); no HIPRT equivalent API

shi-labs/natten.note: libnatten is 100% CUTLASS/CuTe (Ampere/xFormers FNA+FMHA, Hopper wgmma/TMA, Blackwell); naive & CPU CUDA kernels deleted upstream (CHANGELOG: 'Dropped unfused / CPU backends in libnatten'; na{1,2,3}d_{qk,qkrpb,av} removed). CUTLASS does not port to ROCm per PORTING_GUIDE; no non-CUTLASS GPU kernel remains as a first slice. AMD already works via the Flex (torch.nn.attention.flex_attention -> Triton) backend, which is explicitly ROCm-aware (checks.py is_rocm).

shocker-0x15/optix_utility.note: OptiX 9 wrapper; OptiX is NVIDIA RT-core specific, no ROCm equivalent

traveller59/spconv.note: GPU kernels are not static .cu but generated at build time by cumm (FindDefinition/cumm), a CUTLASS-derived pccm/Python codegen GEMM+implicit-conv engine. Build is PCCMExtension, not torch CUDAExtension, so hipify has nothing to translate. Porting spconv == porting cumm's entire CUTLASS/CuTe-style tensor-core (Volta/Turing/Ampere mma.sync + inline-PTX) codegen backend to ROCm; PORTING_GUIDE: CUTLASS does not port, must be reimplemented vs Composable Kernel. Unbounded out-of-scope effort; no authoritative AMD/ROCm spconv port exists upstream.
